UniFirst Corporation (NYSE: UNF) to release Q1 results on Jan 8, 2025, with a conference call at 9:00 am ET.
news.summary: UniFirst Corporation, listed on NYSE as UNF, is set to announce its Fiscal 2025 first quarter results on January 8, 2025, before the market opens. A conference call is scheduled for 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time on the same day to discuss the quarterly financial results, business highlights, and future projections. During the call, the company may address inquiries on various business and financial developments, earnings forecasts, and other related matters. This communication may include undisclosed information.

news.headline: Gabelli Funds' video discusses "Return of Capital" issue in Closed-End Funds, including GAMCO Natural Resources, Gold & Income Trust.
news.summary: A new educational video has been released by Gabelli Funds on Dec. 10, 2024, focusing on the controversial topic of Return of Capital (ROC) in Closed-End Funds. The video, part of The Closed-End Corner series, delves into the significance of ROC for The GAMCO Natural Resources, Gold & Income Trust listed under the ticker symbol GNT on the NYSE. This content sheds light on why ROC, although labeled as a negative term, can actually benefit the trust.

news.headline: Reading Horizons partners with LitLab.ai to enhance early literacy with AI technology, benefiting educators and students globally.
news.summary: A partnership has been formed between Reading Horizons and LitLab.ai, both based in Kaysville, Utah. Reading Horizons, a prominent provider of structured literacy instruction, has teamed up with LitLab.ai, an early reading platform powered by innovative AI technology. This collaboration aims to enhance decoding and fluency skills in students through personalized learning tools. By combining Reading Horizons' proven teaching strategies with LitLab’s advanced technology, educators and students can access engaging and tailored literacy experiences.

news.headline: Survey of 5,000 front-line workers reveals key factors for job satisfaction: flexible schedules and fast payments.
news.summary: In San Francisco on December 10, 2024, WorkWhile released its Annual Flexible Workforce Report which highlights key insights regarding the American workforce. The report, based on a study of over 5,000 front-line workers, emphasized the importance of flexible schedules for employees. Additionally, it revealed that prompt payments within the same or next day are crucial for companies looking to strengthen their workforce. This data provides valuable information for organizations aiming to enhance their workforce strategies and improve employee satisfaction.

news.headline: Goldman Sachs CEO Solomon: The Trump administration is likely to run a pro-growth agenda.
Industrial Filters Market expected to reach $53.4B by 2031 per Meticulous Research. Various types & industries covered.
news.summary: On December 10, 2024, in Redding, California, a recent market study revealed key insights about the Industrial Filters Market. This report covers various types of filters including air, liquid, and gas filters, which are made from materials such as fiberglass, metal, and ceramic. The market includes products like cartridge, drum, HEPA, ULPA, electrostatic, and panel filters and caters to industries such as pharmaceuticals and power. The study showcases significant growth opportunities in the market, with projected numbers indicating a promising future for industrial filters across different sectors.

news.headline: UMAMI Bioworks & Steakholder Foods partner to scale 3D-printed fish fillets, funded by SIIRD grant, advancing to commercialize globally.
news.summary: Singapore, Dec. 10, 2024 - UMAMI Bioworks and Steakholder Foods revealed the results of their two-year R&D collaboration, which was supported by the Singapore-Israel Industrial R&D grant. This partnership, funded with a significant amount, has validated the capability to manufacture 3D-printed cultivated fish items in large quantities. The companies aim to progress towards introducing commercially viable seafood products to the market following the successful feasibility study.

news.headline: Ex-Celsius exec's sentencing cancelled as Alex Mashinsky pleads. Roni Cohen-Pavon pleads guilty to US charges in '23, stays in Israel on bail.
news.summary: In 2023, Roni Cohen-Pavon, former chief revenue officer of Celsius, admitted guilt to US criminal charges. He was granted permission to remain in Israel on bail pending further legal proceedings.

news.headline: Global report reveals top retail transformation initiatives for 2025 across 4 continents and 11 countries.
news.summary: A recent global report spanning four continents and 11 countries has shed light on key areas crucial for store innovation shaping the competitive landscape of the retail sector.

news.headline: Syria's downfall seen as chance for Western allies, watch analysis on Bloomberg.
Global capacitor bank market valued at US$3.6 billion in 2023, expected to reach US$5.5 billion by 2034. Growth driven by renewable energy integration and aging grid infrastructure modernization.
news.summary: The global capacitor bank market was valued at US$ 3.6 Billion in 2023 and is forecasted to grow at a CAGR of 4.1% from 2024 to 2034, reaching US$ 5.5 Billion by 2034. This growth is driven by factors such as increasing demand for renewable energy integration, modernization of aging grid infrastructure, and improvements in power system stability. Capacitor banks are crucial for enhancing power quality, reducing transmission losses, and facilitating more efficient energy distribution. They store and release electrical energy in a controlled manner, providing reactive power compensation, improving power factor, filtering harmonics, and stabilizing voltage levels. Key market drivers include the rise in renewable energy integration and modernization of aging grid infrastructure. Countries committing to clean energy solutions will boost the demand for capacitor banks. Aging infrastructure globally requires modernization, with capacitor banks being essential for stabilizing voltage fluctuations, improving power factor, and mitigating harmonic distortion. Innovative developments in capacitor bank technology, like tuned-filter capacitor banks, are also influencing market growth.

news.headline: Global Gastrointestinal Endoscopy Devices Market valued at USD 12,891.8M in 2024, projected to reach USD 21,413.4M by 2033, with a 5.8% CAGR.
news.summary: The global Gastrointestinal Endoscopy Devices Market was valued at around USD 12,185.1 Million in 2023, projected to reach USD 12,891.8 Million in 2024 and expected to reach approximately USD 21,413.4 Million by 2033, at a compound annual growth rate of about 5.8% during the forecast period. Factors driving market growth include technological advancements, the prevalence of gastrointestinal disorders, a shift towards minimally invasive procedures, an aging population prone to gastrointestinal ailments, expansion of healthcare infrastructure, and regulatory advancements. Key players like Medtronic and FUJIFILM have introduced innovative technologies to enhance diagnostic capabilities and treatment outcomes. The report details market size, growth projections, key segments, and regional scope, covering North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East & Africa, and South & Central America.

news.headline: TraceGains recognized as a top software & technology solution in food industry for ESG readiness and compliance.
news.summary: A networked ecosystem in the food and beverage industry based in Westminster, Colorado, was recognized on Dec. 10, 2024, for establishing a new standard in Environmental, Social, and Governance readiness and compliance. TraceGains, a key player in providing networked solutions for compliance, quality, and innovation, received this honor. The company's efforts have set a commendable benchmark in the sector, highlighting their commitment to upholding high standards. This recognition signifies their dedication to promoting sustainability and responsible practices within the industry.

news.headline: Inventor from Texas creates SANCHEZ SHEETZ to secure bed sheets; eliminates need for readjustment.
news.summary: A resident of Miles, Texas recently shared their innovation, the SANCHEZ SHEETZ, aimed at addressing the common struggle of keeping bed sheets in place. The inventor explained that their creation provides a solution to the issue of having to constantly readjust sheets during the night. The SANCHEZ SHEETZ is designed to offer a simpler way to ensure that the sheets remain in place, potentially leading to improved sleep quality. This inventive solution is expected to offer users a more comfortable and uninterrupted rest.

news.headline: North American Sustainable Refrigeration Council finishes pilot program, training HVACR school faculty with climate-friendly refrigeration.
news.summary: A recent announcement from Mill Valley, California revealed that the North American Sustainable Refrigeration Council has successfully finished a pilot program. The program aimed to provide climate-friendly refrigeration training to faculty members of HVACR schools. This initiative is crucial in promoting sustainable practices within the industry. The training program is expected to benefit a significant number of faculty members across North America.

news.headline: Erin Wood appointed as AssetMark's Senior VP of Advanced Planning, focusing on tailored strategies for financial advisors.

Wood will oversee the launch of AssetMark’s advanced planning services, targeting customized solutions for estate planning, tax optimization, retirement planning, and multi-generational wealth transfer. These offerings aim to aid financial advisors in meeting the changing requirements of investors at various wealth levels. news.summary: Founder of Destination Sitters Yvonne Wonder, based in San Juan Capistrano, California, shared on December 10, 2024, that as the holiday season approaches, numerous families are preparing for air travel to spend the holidays with family. Handling children during flights can be challenging, with an increasing number of families seeking assistance.

news.headline: Genotyping market projected to reach $154.35 billion by 2031, with North America growing from $11.73 billion in 2022 to $37.60 billion by 2028 at a CAGR of 21.4%.

news.headline: FirstEnergy appoints James H. Myers III as President of West Virginia with oversight on engineering and finance. $FE (100)

news.headline: Oshkosh Corporation has been named one of Computerworld's Best Places to Work in IT for 2025, marking the fourth consecutive year.

news.headline: Peptide synthesis market projected to hit $1.12 billion by 2031 due to rising demand for peptide drugs in treating diseases.
news.summary: The peptide synthesis industry is experiencing strong growth, driven by rising demand for peptide-based medications to combat chronic illnesses such as cancer and diabetes. The sector is benefiting from advancements in synthesis techniques, including solid-phase and liquid-phase methods, which are enhancing efficiency and leading to notable improvements in production. This growth is reflective of the escalating need for peptide solutions in the medical field, with the market expected to reach a value of approximately $X billion by the end of the year.

(NYSE: FE) operating as Met-Ed, Penelec, Penn Power, and West Penn Power, has released essential tips to assist customers in managing seasonal heating bills. news.headline: InventHelp inventor from Columbia, S.C. creates FUNBRELLA for outdoor convenience; combines music, phone charging, and lighting.

The inventor expressed a desire for a more convenient way to enhance outdoor settings, such as patio umbrellas, with music, phone charging capabilities, and lighting. The FUNBRELLA boasts a design that eliminates the clutter of multiple devices and offers users a streamlined solution for their entertainment needs. This creation aims to revolutionize outdoor experiences by providing a multifunctional and user-friendly product.

news.headline: Shilpa Ranganathan appointed as Expedia Group's new Chief Product Officer, bringing her expertise in product development.

news.headline: Milliman MedInsight MSSP ACOs saved $549.3M in 2023, outperforming national ACOs. Success in Medicare Shared Savings Program.
news.summary: Milliman MedInsight, a prominent healthcare data and analytics provider, has reported that its clients within the Medicare Shared Savings Program (MSSP) Accountable Care Organizations (ACOs) have achieved a notably higher success rate compared to ACOs nationwide. This success was disclosed on December 10, 2024, in Seattle. The clients of Milliman MedInsight, a trusted leader in the industry, have demonstrated impressive performance, showcasing their effectiveness within the program. This achievement signifies a positive trend in healthcare outcomes, with the MSSP ACO clients outperforming their counterparts across the country.

news.headline: CyTwist's new malware detection engine spots undetectable AI cyberattacks in minutes, instead of months.
news.summary: In a recent cyberattack by AI-generated malware, similar to the one targeting government agencies in France, CyTwist's specialized patented detection engine swiftly identified the attack in a matter of minutes, a process that typically requires months. This highlights the exceptional defense capability of CyTwist against AI-driven threats and innovative attacks.

news.headline: Agilent's PD-L1 IHC 28-8 pharmDx receives EU IVDR certification, Class C. Companion diagnostic assay now certified for sales.
news.summary: Agilent Technologies Inc. revealed the certification of a Class C companion diagnostic In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation for PD-L1 IHC 28-8 pharmDx (Code SK005). The assay, previously CE-IVD-marked for EU sales, now complies with the new EU Regulation for in vitro diagnostic medical devices (IVDR) 1. The certification allows exclusive use with the Agilent Autosta.

news.headline: Mann Packing launches Newman's Own™ Salad Kits, in collaboration with Fresh Del Monte Produce Inc., integrating quality ingredients, benefiting children's well-being. #Philanthropy #NewProducts
news.summary: Fresh Del Monte Produce Inc., operating as Mann Packing, unveils Newman’s Own™ Salad Kits, marking the philanthropic brand's first venture into packaged salad kits. Available through the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol FDP, the product launch signifies a collaboration bridging Fresh Del Monte’s focus on fresh, high-quality offerings and community support with Newman's Own unique charitable business approach. The introduction of this new line showcases Newman’s Own Foundation's dedication to upholding Paul Newman’s legacy, merging quality produce and social responsibility.

news.headline: Tequila Komos opens schoolhouse built from tequila byproducts in Amatitán, Mexico, aims for zero waste with more classrooms to come.
news.summary: In AMATITÁN, Mexico, Tequila Komos and the Komos Foundation recently inaugurated their inaugural schoolhouse made from tequila production byproducts. This initiative showcases the commitment to sustainability of the world's fastest-growing luxury tequila brand. The foundation aims for zero waste and has plans to establish additional classrooms in the region. The schoolhouse is a testament to their dedication to environmental and social responsibility in Amatitán, Mexico.

news.headline: Top titles borrowed on hoopla Digital in 2024: "A Court of Thorns and Roses," Taylor Swift, and "Harry Potter." Over 10,000 US libraries analyzed.
news.summary: In Holland, Ohio, hoopla Digital, an all-in-one digital media app for public libraries, disclosed the top borrowed titles by library patrons in 2024. The data, gathered from over 10,000 U.S. public libraries, highlights the most popular digital content in categories like eBooks, audiobooks, BingePasses, movies, TV shows, music, comics, and manga. This information sheds light on the preferences of library users, showcasing trends in digital media consumption.

news.headline: ACOs using Lightbeam Solutions achieved nearly half a billion in MSSP savings in PY23, surpassing $3 billion in total savings.
news.summary: In Dallas, Lightbeam Health Solutions, a top provider of population health technology, has revealed that Accountable Care Organizations (ACOs) utilizing their solutions achieved close to $500 million in gross savings through the Medicare Shared Savings Program (MSSP) in Performance Year (PY) 2023. Since the program began, Lightbeam has been at the forefront, delivering over $3 billion in total gross MSSP savings and surpassing $5 billion in value generation, solidifying its position as a leading driver of savings and value creation in the industry.

news.headline: "ZIPS Car Wash highlights Abby Ekeler, Lady Vol Volleyball Senior in new episode. Reveals competing with coach dad, favorite campus meal. Dec. 10, 2024."
news.summary: Today in Knoxville, Tennessee, a new episode of "Car Wash Convos™" by ZIPS Car Wash discusses Abby's experience competing with her dad, who is also her coach. Abby shares her favorite meal on campus and responds to a fan question from Instagram. The episode offers an inside look at Abby's perspective on these topics.

news.headline: Legal Process Outsourcing Market projected to reach USD 117.89 billion by 2030, with a 31.4% CAGR growth driven by demand for cost-effective legal services and adoption of technology.
news.summary: The rise in demand for cost-effective legal services is fueling the growth of the Legal Process Outsourcing (LPO) market. This trend enables firms to trim operational costs and tap into specialized skills. The integration of technology such as artificial intelligence and automation is also on the upward trajectory in legal processes, further enhancing efficiency. This industry shift is reflected in the notable increase in LPO services being utilized by firms, showcasing the significant impact of these advancements.

news.headline: PLVision introduces SONiC Lite NOS in Krakow, Poland for cost-effective networking in DC, edge, and campus environments, promoting efficiency and scalability on white-box platforms.
news.summary: In Krakow, Poland, a company called PLVision has introduced SONiC Lite, an enterprise version of the SONiC network operating system. This NOS is designed for cost-effective management and access platforms in various deployments such as data centers, edge, and campus settings. By emphasizing core functionalities and performance, SONiC Lite enables enterprises, regardless of size, to establish efficient and scalable networks using less advanced white-box networking platforms. This strategy promotes vendor neutrality and helps optimize the overall total cost of ownership (TCO).
